tagline: ZPE-Mocap is Zero-Point Encoding's motion-capture compression and retrieval sector. This repo contains a deterministic Python reference implementation plus an imported synthetic-corpus proof bundle dated 2026-02-20. Every public claim below is limited to that synthetic evidence. No Blender runtime pass, no CMU commercialization-safe closure, and no clean-clone verification are claimed here.

authorityState.summary: Backend truth backend=python Python reference implementation; no compiled runtime authority is claimed here.

provedNow: ZPE-Mocap is Zero-Point Encoding's motion-capture compression and retrieval sector. This repo contains a deterministic Python reference implementation plus an imported synthetic-corpus proof bundle dated 2026-02-20. Every public claim below is limited to that synthetic evidence. No Blender runtime pass, no CMU commercialization-safe closure, and no clean-clone verification are claimed here.
A deterministic mocap compression and retrieval reference stack backed by a synthetic corpus with preserved proof lineage.
Synthetic-corpus compression ratio, joint-angle fidelity, positional fidelity, search ranking, and query-latency metrics in the wave1 bundle.
Synthetic-corpus mean compression ratio from the wave1 benchmark artifact.
Synthetic query latency p95 from the wave1 benchmark.
The reference implementation is documented as deterministic on the shipped synthetic corpus.
explicitNonClaims: No throughput benchmark is promoted. The only performance numbers currently promoted are synthetic-corpus compression and query-latency metrics from the wave1 bundle.
No public ML workbook is promoted for ZPE-Mocap at this time. All promoted evidence remains in the local wave1 proof bundle under proofs/artifacts/2026-02-20_zpe_mocap_wave1/ .
No public ML workbook has been published for this repo; evidence is local to the wave1 bundle.
No. The promoted metrics in the README are synthetic-corpus results from the 2026-02-20 wave1 bundle. CMU real-corpus gates have not been run in this repo.
Not yet. Blender runtime proof remains unpromoted and is explicitly treated as a gap in the current docs.
No. This remains a staging repo whose promoted authority is bounded to the current synthetic bundle and explicit non-claims.
No. Docs can clarify truth and improve auditability, but they cannot replace missing CMU, Blender, or clean-clone evidence.
